🌅 Market News Digest
[Jul 2–3, 2026 EST]

🔥 Top Stories
• US jobs miss jolts rates, gold, FX — softer June payrolls pushed Treasury yields and the dollar lower, while gold jumped toward record highs
• Trump doubles down on crypto/AI and Iran — said U.S. must lead in crypto and AI, denied wrongdoing on $1.4B crypto gains, and kept pressure on Iran talks
• Hormuz tensions ease, oil steadies then slips — transit normalizes and talks continue; crude held near $68/bbl before easing on supply relief
• PJM grid emergency — PJM issued maximum-generation/load alerts and ordered all offline generation back online amid heat
• Canada backs new west-coast pipeline — Ottawa, Alberta and industry agreed on a new crude pipeline route, boosting oil-sands/export hopes

⛽ Oil & Energy
• Hormuz flows recovering; Qatar sent first LNG tanker through in a week as ceasefire held
• Canada/Alberta advanced a new pipeline plan; Alberta wants higher oil-sands output
• TotalEnergies is offering Iraqi oil to Asian buyers as supply swells
• Saudi crude exports recovered to ~90% of pre-war levels

📊 Markets & Macro
• US futures and global equities mostly firmer; Asia mixed with Japan/Korea swings tied to tech weakness
• Gold surged to the $4,100–$4,200 area; silver also spiked as rate-cut bets built
• Eurozone PMIs held at expansionary levels; France stayed weak while Germany and Italy improved
• Japan PMIs improved; Rengo wage gains came in at 5.01%, still supportive for BOJ normalization
• China services PMI eased but stayed solid; PBoC set a stronger yuan fix and injected 63B yuan via repos

🌍 Geopolitical
• US-Iran talks remain front and center; Iran warned against interference in Hormuz and said it will respond if commitments are broken
• Ukraine hit Russian airfields in Crimea; Belgorod industrial site also reportedly burned after strikes
• Poland will not transfer remaining MiG-29s to Ukraine after the swap plan collapsed
• Turkey pressed for CAATSA/F-35 relief and kept up hard-line rhetoric on Israel

🏛️ Regulatory & Policy
• ECB’s Moulin signaled no new hiking cycle; Lagarde said ~30 countries want ECB repo-line access
• France’s finance minister signaled next year’s budget likely and a push to get deficit below 5%
• Japan officials reiterated readiness to act on FX and keep policy supportive as yields rise
• China may revise EV tax exemptions; UK/BoE survey still shows sticky wage/price expectations

🏢 Corporate
• Michael Burry disclosed a short on Micron
• Meta said AI agent development is slower than expected and is weighing Samsung for custom AI chips
• Anthropic is tightening access/safeguards around Claude; Alibaba also moved to ban Claude Code internally
• Crusoe is reportedly seeking $3B funding; Kuaishou filed for a $3B Kling AI round
• Getty Images was downgraded to CCC+ on liquidity concerns after the Shutterstock deal fell apart
